action.content: Write a 30-line poem with short sentences without any comma. Each line should contain exactly one sentence. Make sure that you put the right punctuation at the end of each line. Your entire response should contain the poem only.
action.label: context

action.content:
The sun rises high above the hill.  
Birds sing songs in the morning air.  
A gentle breeze blows through the trees.  
Leaves dance softly on the ground.  
A child runs across the green grass.  
Flowers bloom in bright colors.  
The river flows with a steady sound.  
Fish swim beneath the water’s surface.  
A boat glides quietly on the stream.  
Clouds drift lazily across the sky.  
Rain begins to fall from above.  
Drops hit the roof with a tap.  
The ground becomes wet and shiny.  
An umbrella opens over a head.  
People hurry under the gray sky.  
Thunder rumbles deep in the distance.  
Lightning flashes across the horizon.  
The storm passes quickly by.  
Sunlight returns to warm the earth.  
A rainbow arches over the field.  
Children laugh and play outside again.  
Evening brings a cool, fresh air.  
Stars appear one by one at night.  
The moon shines bright and silver.  
A fire crackles in the hearth.  
Candles flicker on the table.  
Silence fills the quiet room.  
Dreams begin to form in sleep.  
Morning comes once more to start.
action.label: response
